meta.one_liner: Chronic, immune-mediated skin disease driven by the IL-23 → Th17 → IL-17 axis; a maturing, ~$21B market where injectable IL-23/IL-17 biologics dominate efficacy and the next battle is oral, biologic-grade therapy.
meta.coverage_note: Pipeline and mechanism data from ClinicalTrials.gov (901 interventional studies) and Open Targets (167 drug candidates, 85 mechanism groups); approved-drug/label data from openFDA. Market size, product sales, epidemiology, deals and catalysts are from cited web sources (company filings/press releases, GBD 2021, peer-reviewed prevalence studies, named analyst reports). Sales figures are FY2024 unless noted. Some emerging-asset details (e.g. Oruka programs) have thin public data and are flagged.

headline_stats.label: US prevalence
headline_stats.detail: ~3% of US adults ≥20y; plaque type ~80–90% of cases

headline_stats.label: Global prevalence
headline_stats.detail: GBD 2021 counts ~43M; WHO estimates >60M; higher-end figures include all severities

headline_stats.label: Moderate-to-severe
headline_stats.detail: share of patients eligible for systemic/biologic therapy

headline_stats.label: Approved systemic/biologic classes
headline_stats.detail: TNF, IL-12/23, IL-17, IL-23, PDE4, TYK2 — plus first oral IL-23R (2026)

headline_stats.label: Active clinical-stage assets
headline_stats.detail: 188 assets seen at Phase 3, 266 at Phase 2 across trials

headline_stats.label: Best-in-class skin clearance
headline_stats.detail: at wk16 for IL-17/IL-23 biologics vs ~5% placebo

overview.definition: Plaque psoriasis (psoriasis vulgaris) is a chronic, relapsing, immune-mediated inflammatory skin disease characterized by well-demarcated, raised, erythematous plaques with silvery scale, typically on the extensor surfaces (elbows, knees), scalp, and lower back. It is the most common form of psoriasis, accounting for roughly 80–90% of cases.
overview.pathophysiology: The disease is driven by a dysregulated adaptive immune response in genetically predisposed individuals. Activated dendritic cells release IL-23, which sustains pathogenic T-helper-17 (Th17) cells; these secrete IL-17A/F and IL-22, driving keratinocyte hyperproliferation, neutrophil recruitment, and a self-amplifying inflammatory loop. TNF-α and the intracellular kinase TYK2 (upstream of IL-23 signaling) are additional validated nodes. This 'IL-23/IL-17 axis' is the organizing principle of both the biology and the drug landscape.
overview.state_of_play: Psoriasis is one of the great success stories of translational immunology: over two decades the field moved from broad immunosuppression (methotrexate, cyclosporine) to TNF inhibitors, then to highly selective IL-17 and IL-23 antibodies that routinely deliver PASI 90 (90% skin clearance) — a bar unthinkable 20 years ago. The clinical problem of 'can we clear skin?' is largely solved for patients who can access biologics. The frontier has shifted to three questions: (1) can an ORAL drug match biologic efficacy and tolerability (the oral TYK2 and oral IL-23R race — the March 2026 approval of icotrokinra/Icotyde, the first oral IL-23 receptor antagonist, is the landmark event); (2) can therapy be made more durable and convenient (half-life-extended antibodies, less-frequent dosing); and (3) how does the market absorb the coming biosimilar erosion (Stelara's cliff hit in 2025; Cosentyx/Taltz follow ~2028–2029). The strategic tension is no longer efficacy but access, route, and price.

epidemiology.summary: Psoriasis affects an estimated 2–3% of adults worldwide, with marked geographic variation (higher in high-SDI regions — Western Europe, North America). Prevalence has risen over three decades as populations age and diagnosis improves. Plaque psoriasis is the dominant subtype. The commercially and clinically decisive segmentation is severity: the ~20–25% with moderate-to-severe disease drive nearly all systemic and biologic use.

segments.name: Mild (topical-managed)
segments.note: Limited body-surface-area involvement; managed with topical corticosteroids, vitamin D analogs, and combinations. Large population, low per-patient drug spend.

segments.name: Moderate-to-severe (systemic-eligible)
segments.note: ≥3 palms / ≥10% BSA, or special-site or high-impact disease. The economic engine: candidates for biologics, oral systemics (apremilast, deucravacitinib), and phototherapy.

segments.name: Difficult-to-treat sites
segments.note: Scalp, nail, genital, palmoplantar involvement — higher QoL impact, often undertreated; a differentiation battleground (e.g. icotrokinra scalp/genital data).

segments.name: Psoriatic arthritis overlap
segments.note: Up to a third develop psoriatic arthritis; drives choice toward agents with joint efficacy (IL-17, IL-23, TNF, TYK2).

epidemiology.demographics: Bimodal onset (peaks ~20–30y and ~50–60y); roughly equal sex distribution. Strong genetic component (HLA-C*06:02) and heritability. Key comorbidities — psoriatic arthritis, cardiometabolic disease, obesity, depression — add to burden and inform the emerging interest in metabolic agents (GLP-1) as adjuncts.
epidemiology.unmet_need: Despite excellent biologics, real-world unmet need persists: (1) most patients still receive no systemic therapy due to access, cost, or needle aversion — an oral, biologic-grade option addresses a huge underserved base; (2) durability/relapse after discontinuation; (3) difficult sites (scalp, nails, genitals) and PsA prevention; (4) safety concerns limiting JAK-class uptake; (5) the ~30–40% who do not reach complete clearance (PASI 100) on current agents.

patient_journey.description: Clinical diagnosis by appearance; often delayed or self-managed. Genetic and environmental triggers (infection, stress, trauma — Koebner phenomenon).
First-line for mild disease: corticosteroids + vitamin D analogs (± nonsteroidal tapinarof/roflumilast). Many patients cycle here for years.
Moderate-to-severe or topical-refractory disease steps up to phototherapy, oral systemics (methotrexate, apremilast, deucravacitinib), or biologics.
IL-23 or IL-17 biologics for durable clearance; switching between classes on loss of response; matching to comorbidities (PsA, IBD).
Chronic, lifelong management; relapse on discontinuation; evolving question of drug holidays and disease modification.

biology.summary: Psoriasis biology converges on a single dominant circuit: dendritic-cell-derived IL-23 licenses pathogenic Th17 cells, whose effector cytokines (IL-17A, IL-17F, IL-22) drive keratinocyte activation, neutrophilic inflammation, and epidermal hyperplasia. TNF-α amplifies the loop, and the intracellular kinase TYK2 transduces IL-23 (and type-I-IFN) signaling — making each step a validated, sequentially drugged target. This clean, well-mapped pathway is why psoriasis has the most target-validated pipeline in immunology.

pathways.name: IL-23 / Th17 axis
pathways.role: Central disease driver. IL-23 (p19/p40) maintains Th17 cells; blocking IL-23 (p19) collapses the downstream cascade with high durability — the most successful modern target.

pathways.name: IL-17 effector signaling
pathways.role: IL-17A/F act on keratinocytes via IL-17RA to drive the clinical plaque; direct blockade gives the fastest, deepest skin clearance.

pathways.name: TNF-α signaling
pathways.role: Amplifies inflammation; the original biologic target, now a mature/biosimilar class, still relevant for PsA and cross-indication.

pathways.name: TYK2 / JAK-STAT
pathways.role: TYK2 transduces IL-23 and IL-12 signaling intracellularly; selective allosteric TYK2 inhibition (deucravacitinib) enables oral targeting of the axis without broad JAK toxicity.

pathways.name: PDE4 / cAMP
pathways.role: PDE4 inhibition raises intracellular cAMP, dampening pro-inflammatory cytokine output — the mechanism of oral apremilast and topical roflumilast.

pathways.name: IL-36 / innate
pathways.role: IL-36 signaling drives the pustular/innate arm (pivotal in generalized pustular psoriasis; spesolimab); less central to plaque disease but mechanistically distinct.

pathways.name: AhR & retinoid/vitamin-D nuclear receptors
pathways.role: Aryl hydrocarbon receptor (tapinarof) and VDR/RAR agonism normalize keratinocyte differentiation — the basis of modern nonsteroidal topicals.

targets.name: Interleukin-23 subunit p19
targets.rationale: Apex driver of the pathogenic circuit; p19 blockade gives durable clearance with infrequent dosing.

targets.name: Interleukin-17A
targets.rationale: Key effector cytokine; direct blockade gives fastest/deepest clearance.

targets.name: Interleukin-17F
targets.rationale: Co-driver with IL-17A; dual A/F blockade (bimekizumab) raises the efficacy ceiling.

targets.name: IL-17 receptor A
targets.rationale: Blocks signaling of multiple IL-17 family members (brodalumab).

targets.name: Tyrosine kinase 2
targets.rationale: Intracellular node for IL-23/IL-12; allosteric (pseudokinase-domain) inhibition enables oral, selective targeting.

targets.name: IL-12/23 subunit p40
targets.rationale: Shared p40 blockade (ustekinumab) hits both IL-12 and IL-23; first-generation validated target.

targets.name: Tumor necrosis factor-α
targets.rationale: Amplifier cytokine; foundational biologic target, now biosimilar-eroded.

targets.name: Phosphodiesterase 4B
targets.rationale: cAMP regulator; oral (apremilast) and topical (roflumilast) anti-inflammatory.

targets.name: Aryl hydrocarbon receptor
targets.rationale: Modulates keratinocyte differentiation and Th17 balance; basis of tapinarof.

targets.name: IL-36 receptor
targets.rationale: Innate/pustular arm; spesolimab validated in GPP.

subtypes.name: Chronic plaque (vulgaris)
subtypes.description: The dominant phenotype targeted by nearly all systemic development.
subtypes.biomarker: HLA-C*06:02 (associated; also predicts some IL-12/23 response)

subtypes.name: Scalp / nail / genital / palmoplantar
subtypes.description: Site-specific, high-impact variants used for differentiation.

subtypes.name: Pustular (incl. GPP)
subtypes.description: Innate IL-36-driven; distinct biology and drugs (spesolimab).

standard_of_care.paradigm: Treatment is stratified by severity and site. MILD disease: topical corticosteroids ± vitamin D analogs, with nonsteroidal tapinarof (AhR) and roflumilast (PDE4) as steroid-sparing options. MODERATE-TO-SEVERE: step to phototherapy or systemic therapy. Oral systemics — methotrexate (cheap, legacy), apremilast (PDE4), and deucravacitinib (oral TYK2, the modern oral of choice) — sit below biologics. BIOLOGICS are the efficacy standard: IL-23 inhibitors (risankizumab, guselkumab, tildrakizumab) and IL-17 inhibitors (secukinumab, ixekizumab, bimekizumab, brodalumab) deliver PASI 90 in the majority; TNF inhibitors and IL-12/23 (ustekinumab) are older lines now facing biosimilars. The 2026 arrival of oral IL-23R antagonist icotrokinra (Icotyde) begins to blur the oral/biologic line at the top of the funnel.

lines.note: Most patients; low drug cost. Nonsteroidals reduce steroid-related atrophy.
Deucravacitinib is displacing apremilast as the preferred modern oral; methotrexate remains a global cost anchor.
Preferred for durability and infrequent (q8–12w) dosing; excellent long-term safety.
Fastest/deepest clearance; bimekizumab (dual IL-17A/F) sets the PASI-100 ceiling. Caution in IBD; candidiasis risk.
Mature classes, biosimilar-eroded; still used for PsA and in pregnancy (certolizumab).
Approved Mar 2026; oral peptide positioned for first-line systemic use, especially needle-averse and difficult-site patients.

top_products.note: Market leader; ~45% of US psoriasis biologic new-Rx share in Q4 2024. Cross-indication franchise; ~$17.5B in 2025.
Global sales ~$10.4B in 2024 but declining ~14% as US biosimilars launched from Jan 2025 (loss of exclusivity).
~$6.1B, +24% cc; broad label. US core patent ~2029.
+16.6% YoY; J&J's designated growth engine to offset Stelara erosion.
Oral incumbent; sales down ~3–4% on price/competition from Sotyktu.
Dual IL-17A/F; highest PASI-100 rates. Fast launch — €607M ($716M) 2024 → ~$2.6B 2025.
First oral selective TYK2; US demand +21% in 2024 (exact FY revenue not separately disclosed here). PsA approval added 2026.
Foundational TNF; heavily biosimilar-eroded since 2023 in the US. Psoriasis now a small fraction of use.
First-in-class oral IL-23R antagonist, approved Mar 18 2026; once-daily pill positioned for first-line systemic use.

class_share.basis: approx. share of psoriasis biologic value, 2024 (Skyrizi+Tremfya+Ilumya)
approx. share of psoriasis biologic value, 2024 (Cosentyx+Taltz+Bimzelx+Siliq)

guidelines.name: AAD-NPF Guidelines of Care for Psoriasis (2019–2021)
guidelines.note: US joint American Academy of Dermatology / National Psoriasis Foundation guidelines spanning biologics, systemics, topicals, phototherapy, and comorbidity screening.

guidelines.name: NPF treat-to-target
guidelines.note: Target of ≤1% BSA (or PASI response) within 3 months of therapy initiation.

moa_landscape.rationale: IL-23 is the apex cytokine sustaining pathogenic Th17 cells; blocking it upstream collapses the whole IL-17 cascade with exceptional durability.
moa_landscape.mechanism: Monoclonal antibodies bind the p19 subunit of IL-23, preventing receptor engagement and Th17 maintenance.

moa_landscape.rationale: Deliver the durable, safe IL-23 mechanism in an ORAL format — addressing the huge needle-averse / access-limited moderate-to-severe base at the top of the funnel.
moa_landscape.mechanism: Targeted oral peptide selectively blocks the IL-23 receptor, disrupting IL-23 signaling without a systemic antibody.
cons: skin clearance modestly below best injectables

moa_landscape.rationale: Direct blockade of the key effector cytokine gives the fastest and deepest skin clearance.
moa_landscape.mechanism: mAbs neutralize IL-17A, blocking keratinocyte activation and neutrophilic inflammation.

moa_landscape.rationale: Neutralizing both IL-17A and IL-17F raises the efficacy ceiling to the highest PASI-100 rates seen.
moa_landscape.mechanism: Single mAb binds both IL-17A and IL-17F homo/heterodimers.
pros: superiority vs adalimumab, secukinumab, ustekinumab in head-to-heads

moa_landscape.rationale: Blocking the shared receptor inhibits signaling of multiple IL-17 family members.
cons: boxed warning for suicidal ideation/behavior (brodalumab) — REMS

moa_landscape.rationale: First-generation axis blockade hitting shared p40 of IL-12 and IL-23; proof-of-concept for the whole modern era.
cons: superseded on efficacy by selective IL-23

moa_landscape.rationale: TYK2 transduces IL-23/IL-12 signaling intracellularly; allosteric (non-ATP-site) inhibition gives oral, selective axis blockade without broad JAK toxicity.
moa_landscape.mechanism: Small molecules bind the regulatory pseudokinase domain, locking TYK2 in an inactive state.
pros: cleaner safety than pan-JAK (no boxed JAK warning class effect to date)
cons: skin clearance historically below top biologics (first-gen)

moa_landscape.rationale: Bring the potent IL-17 effector mechanism into an oral format.
moa_landscape.mechanism: Orally bioavailable small molecules / macrocyclic peptides that block IL-17A signaling.
pros: potential biologic-like efficacy in a pill
cons: Lilly deprioritized lead DC-806, pivoting to follow-on — class execution risk

moa_landscape.rationale: Amplifier cytokine of the inflammatory loop; the original biologic breakthrough.
Raising intracellular cAMP broadly dampens pro-inflammatory cytokine output — an oral or topical non-biologic option.
moa_landscape.mechanism: Inhibit PDE4, elevating cAMP in immune cells and keratinocytes.

moa_landscape.class: Nonsteroidal nuclear-receptor topicals (AhR / retinoid / vitamin D)
moa_landscape.rationale: Normalize keratinocyte differentiation and local immune tone — steroid-sparing topical control for mild disease and maintenance.
moa_landscape.mechanism: AhR agonism (tapinarof), retinoid-receptor agonism (tazarotene), and VDR agonism (calcipotriene).
cons: modest as monotherapy in severe disease

moa_landscape.rationale: Broad immunomodulation; the global cost anchor and pre-biologic legacy.
Targets the innate/pustular arm; transformative in generalized pustular psoriasis, mechanistically distinct from plaque disease.
cons: limited role in ordinary plaque psoriasis

moa_landscape.rationale: Engineer IL-23/IL-17 antibodies for far less frequent dosing (potentially 1–2×/year) to defend the injectable franchise against orals and biosimilars.
moa_landscape.mechanism: Fc/affinity engineering to extend half-life and lower dose frequency.

moa_landscape.rationale: Obesity worsens psoriasis and blunts biologic response; incretin agonists are in trials as weight/inflammation-directed adjuncts.
moa_landscape.mechanism: GLP-1 / dual GIP-GLP-1 receptor agonism (semaglutide, tirzepatide).
cons: not a psoriasis therapy per se

moa_landscape.rationale: Oral small-molecule immunomodulation via A3AR; a differentiated non-cytokine oral mechanism in late development.
moa_landscape.mechanism: Agonism of the A3 adenosine receptor on inflammatory cells.

moa_landscape.rationale: Broad JAK blockade is efficacious but carries class safety warnings; largely bypassed in plaque psoriasis in favor of selective TYK2.
moa_landscape.mechanism: ATP-competitive inhibition of JAK family kinases (tofacitinib, others).
cons: boxed warnings (thrombosis, MACE, malignancy) — unfavorable risk/benefit vs selective TYK2 in a skin-only disease

moa_landscape.rationale: Early biologic targeting memory T cells; a strategic signal of a failed approach.
moa_landscape.mechanism: LFA-3/Fc fusion depletes CD2+ memory-effector T cells.
cons: modest efficacy; withdrawn from market. Efalizumab (anti-CD11a) withdrawn for PML risk

pipeline.summary: The clinical-stage landscape is unusually deep and target-validated. Approved anchors span TNF, IL-12/23, IL-17, IL-23, PDE4, TYK2, and (from 2026) oral IL-23R. The active frontier is overwhelmingly ORAL: next-gen oral TYK2 inhibitors (zasocitinib, ESK-001) racing to match biologics, oral IL-23R peptides (icotrokinra approved; Protagonist/Oruka behind), and oral IL-17 antagonists (ex-DICE). A parallel thread is convenience: half-life-extended antibodies for 1–2×/year dosing. Assets below are the decision-relevant, de-duplicated set (approved + notable clinical) drawn from ~180 clinical-stage programs seen across 901 trials and Open Targets; formulation/biosimilar variants are collapsed.

assets.name: mirikizumab
indications: plaque psoriasis (Ph3 positive, not commercialized in pso)

assets.name: DC-806 / follow-on
assets.mechanism: oral small-molecule IL-17A antagonist (lead DC-806 deprioritized; follow-on pursued)

assets.name: calcipotriene
assets.mechanism: topical vitamin D receptor agonist (often w/ betamethasone)

assets.name: tofacitinib
assets.mechanism: pan-JAK inhibitor (boxed warnings; deprioritized in plaque)

companies.name: AbbVie
companies.positioning: Immunology leader post-Humira; Skyrizi (IL-23) is the psoriasis market leader and, with Rinvoq, the core growth engine (guided ~$31B combined by 2027). Deep cross-indication franchise.

companies.name: Johnson & Johnson
companies.positioning: Broadest psoriasis franchise: Stelara (now biosimilar-eroded), Tremfya (growth driver), and the first oral IL-23R antagonist Icotyde (icotrokinra) — uniquely spanning legacy, growth, and next-gen oral.

deals.note: Icotyde (icotrokinra) approved — first oral IL-23R antagonist; peptide licensed originally from Protagonist Therapeutics.

companies.name: Novartis
companies.positioning: IL-17 pioneer with Cosentyx (~$6B), the broadest IL-17 label; defending against Bimzelx and a ~2029 patent cliff.

companies.name: Eli Lilly
companies.positioning: Taltz (IL-17) incumbent; betting on ORAL immunology via the DICE acquisition (oral IL-17) and metabolic-immunology crossover (tirzepatide).

deals.note: Acquired for oral IL-17 antagonists (DC-806/DC-853); lead later deprioritized in favor of a follow-on.

companies.name: UCB
companies.positioning: Bimzelx (dual IL-17A/F) is the efficacy-ceiling biologic — fastest immunology launch, crossing blockbuster in 2025; broad label (pso, PsA, axSpA, HS).

companies.name: Bristol Myers Squibb
companies.positioning: Category creator of oral selective TYK2 with Sotyktu (deucravacitinib); expanding into psoriatic arthritis (approved 2026). Faces next-gen TYK2 competition.

companies.name: Takeda
companies.positioning: Chasing best-in-class oral TYK2 with zasocitinib (TAK-279); Phase 3 positive and beat Sotyktu head-to-head; FDA filing planned 2026; guided $3–6B peak.

deals.note: Acquired Nimbus Lakshmi to obtain TAK-279 (zasocitinib) after positive Phase 2.

companies.name: Amgen
companies.positioning: Otezla (oral PDE4) incumbent under pricing/competitive pressure; also a leading Stelara-biosimilar player (Wezlana).

companies.name: Alumis
companies.positioning: Next-gen oral TYK2 (ESK-001) in Phase 3 ONWARD; merged with ACELYRIN (2025) to bolster late-stage immunology scale and cash.

deals.note: All-stock merger creating a late-stage immunology company; ESK-001 Ph3 topline expected 1Q2026.

companies.name: Oruka Therapeutics
companies.positioning: Convenience-focused: half-life-extended anti-IL-23 (ORKA-001) and anti-IL-17 (ORKA-002) engineered for 1–2×/year dosing — aiming to out-convenience biosimilars. Early clinical; limited public disclosure.

companies.name: Arcutis Biotherapeutics
companies.positioning: Topical innovator; Zoryve (roflumilast) is a leading nonsteroidal topical for plaque psoriasis, scalp, and beyond.

market.summary: The psoriasis drug market was ~$21B in 2024 and is forecast to reach ~$39B by 2030 (10.9% CAGR per Grand View Research), driven by biologic adoption, label expansion, and new orals. Biologics account for ~46% of value and North America ~39%. The market's defining dynamics are (1) intense IL-23 vs IL-17 competition at the premium end (Skyrizi leading), (2) a wave of biosimilar erosion — Stelara from 2025, Humira ongoing, Cosentyx/Taltz ~2028–2029 — which compresses value even as volumes rise, and (3) an oral-therapy inflection (TYK2, oral IL-23R) that could expand the treated population by pulling systemic therapy earlier and to needle-averse patients.
drivers: Continued shift from topicals/conventional systemics to biologics and targeted orals
Label expansion into PsA, HS, and pediatric/adolescent use
Oral TYK2 and oral IL-23R broadening the systemic-treated population
Rising diagnosed prevalence in aging, higher-SDI populations
Offsetting headwind: biosimilar erosion of TNF, IL-12/23, then IL-17 franchises
market.pricing_access: Biologics carry high US list prices (tens of thousands/yr) with substantial rebating; access is gated by step therapy through conventional systemics and payer formularies. Biosimilars (Stelara, Humira, and eventually IL-17s) are the primary price-compression force. BMS has notably discounted Sotyktu's list price to compete on access. An approved oral (icotrokinra) with a favorable convenience/safety profile could shift step-therapy economics by displacing older orals and pulling systemic treatment earlier.

opportunity.title: Biologic-grade efficacy in a pill
opportunity.rationale: The largest prize: an oral matching IL-23/IL-17 clearance with clean safety captures the needle-averse and access-limited majority of moderate-to-severe patients. Next-gen oral TYK2 (zasocitinib) and oral IL-23R (icotrokinra) are the front-runners.

opportunity.title: Convenience / durability differentiation
opportunity.rationale: As IL-23/IL-17 antibodies commoditize toward biosimilars, ultra-infrequent dosing (1–2×/year, half-life-extended antibodies) or true drug-free remission becomes the differentiator.

opportunity.title: Complete clearance (PASI 100)
opportunity.rationale: ~30–40% still fall short of total clearance; dual IL-17A/F (bimekizumab) raised the ceiling and defines the aspiration for next agents.

opportunity.title: Difficult sites & PsA prevention
opportunity.rationale: Scalp, nail, genital, palmoplantar disease and preventing progression to psoriatic arthritis are under-addressed differentiators (icotrokinra scalp/genital data; IL-23/IL-17/TYK2 joint benefit).

opportunity.title: Post-cliff value migration
opportunity.rationale: Stelara/Humira/Cosentyx/Taltz losses of exclusivity free share and payer budget — whoever owns the best oral or most convenient biologic captures the switchers.

catalysts.significance: First oral IL-23 receptor antagonist — opens a new oral, first-line systemic category and pressures both orals (Otezla, Sotyktu) and injectable biologics.
Expands the oral TYK2 franchise into joints, strengthening its position vs orals and biologics.
Key test of next-gen oral TYK2 efficacy/safety; a strong result validates the higher-exposure TYK2 thesis.
Phase 3 positive and superior to Sotyktu head-to-head; filing sets up a potential best-in-class oral TYK2 launch (guided $3–6B peak).

catalysts.company: multiple (Amgen, Teva, Sandoz/Samsung Bioepis, Celltrion, Biocon)
catalysts.significance: Loss of exclusivity from Jan 2025; multibillion-dollar value migration and payer-budget release toward newer agents.

catalysts.event: Taltz (~2028) and Cosentyx (~2029) US patent expiries
catalysts.significance: IL-17 class begins its biosimilar cliff; accelerates the shift of premium value to orals and next-gen biologics.

catalysts.event: Icotrokinra head-to-head vs ustekinumab & continued ICONIC readouts
catalysts.significance: First trial designed to show an oral pill superior to an injectable biologic — would be a paradigm signal for oral-first treatment.

evidence.summary: Psoriasis has an unusually clean efficacy hierarchy anchored on PASI response (% patients achieving 75/90/100% skin-lesion improvement) at week ~16. Dual IL-17A/F and the best IL-23 agents sit at the top (PASI 90 ~60–70%+), IL-17A and IL-23 next, oral TYK2 in the middle, and PDE4/conventional systemics lower. Head-to-head trials (a hallmark of this field) repeatedly rank newer selective agents above older ones (e.g., bimekizumab > secukinumab/adalimumab/ustekinumab; risankizumab > ustekinumab/adalimumab; deucravacitinib > apremilast; zasocitinib > deucravacitinib). Safety differentiators: IL-17 candidiasis and IBD caution; brodalumab suicidality boxed warning; JAK-class warnings that selective TYK2 has so far avoided.

landmark_trials.name: UltIMMa-1/2
landmark_trials.result: ~75% vs ~5% placebo and superior to ustekinumab

landmark_trials.name: BE VIVID / BE RADIANT
landmark_trials.result: Highest complete-clearance rates; superior to ustekinumab and secukinumab

landmark_trials.name: ICONIC-LEAD / ADVANCE / TOTAL
landmark_trials.result: ~65–74% clear/almost-clear — industry-leading for an oral, durable to wk52

landmark_trials.name: Zasocitinib Phase 3 (plaque psoriasis)
landmark_trials.result: ~52–61% PASI 90; superior to Sotyktu in head-to-head

strengths: Exceptionally well-validated biology (IL-23/IL-17 axis) — the deepest target-validated pipeline in immunology
Multiple high-efficacy classes already approved; PASI 90 routine
Large, growing, well-diagnosed patient base with clear severity segmentation
weaknesses: Efficacy problem largely solved — differentiation is now incremental and crowded
Chronic, relapsing disease with no cure/durable remission after stopping
Access, cost, and injection burden leave much of the eligible population untreated
opportunities: Oral biologic-grade therapy (TYK2, IL-23R) to expand and pull-forward the treated population
Value migration from the 2025–2029 biosimilar cliff (Stelara → Humira → IL-17s)
threats: Biosimilar erosion compressing market value even as volumes grow
Class-safety overhangs (JAK warnings; IL-17 IBD/candidiasis) shaping positioning

sources.title: AbbVie Reports Full-Year and Fourth-Quarter 2024 Financial Results
sources.note: Skyrizi FY2024 net revenue $11.718B; ~$17.5B in 2025.

sources.name: GBD 2021 (PMC)
sources.title: Global Trends and Future Predictions of Psoriasis Burden: Insights from GBD 2021
sources.note: Global prevalence ~23M (1990) → ~43M (2021); higher in high-SDI regions.

sources.name: JAMA Dermatology (PMC)
sources.title: Psoriasis Prevalence in Adults in the United States
sources.note: ~7.55M US adults ≥20y (~3%); ~21.5% report moderate-to-severe.

sources.name: Johnson & Johnson
sources.title: Johnson & Johnson Form 10-K FY2024 (immunology sales)
sources.note: Tremfya $3.67B (+16.6%); Stelara ~$10.4B global, declining on biosimilars.

sources.name: DelveInsight / drugpatentwatch
sources.title: Taltz market ascent; Taltz patent expiry
sources.note: Taltz FY2024 ~$3.2B; US patent expiry ~2028.

sources.name: Grand View Research (market trends)
sources.title: Secukinumab (Cosentyx): Navigating the Patent Cliff
sources.note: Cosentyx ~$6.14B (2024); US core patent ~2029.

sources.name: Amgen
sources.title: Amgen Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Financial Results
sources.note: Otezla FY2024 ~$2.2B; down ~3–4% on price/competition.

sources.name: FiercePharma / UCB
sources.title: UCB Bimzelx crosses blockbuster threshold

sources.name: Bristol Myers Squibb
sources.title: Bristol Myers Squibb Form 10-K FY2024 (Sotyktu)
sources.note: Sotyktu US revenues +21% in 2024; oral selective TYK2; list-price discounting for access.

sources.name: Johnson & Johnson
sources.title: FDA approval of ICOTYDE (icotrokinra) — first oral IL-23R antagonist
sources.note: Approved Mar 18 2026 for moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis (≥12y, ≥40kg).

sources.name: BioPharma Dive
sources.title: Takeda says $4B TYK2 drug (zasocitinib) succeeds in large psoriasis studies
sources.note: TAK-279 Ph3 positive (PASI 90 ~52–61%); beat Sotyktu head-to-head; filings 2026; from $4B Nimbus deal (2022).

sources.name: Eli Lilly / BioPharma Dive
sources.title: Lilly to Acquire DICE Therapeutics ($2.4B)
sources.note: 2023 $2.4B acquisition for oral IL-17 antagonists (DC-806/DC-853); lead later deprioritized.

sources.name: Alumis
sources.title: Alumis and ACELYRIN to Merge; ESK-001 Phase 3 ONWARD
sources.note: All-stock merger (2025); ESK-001 oral TYK2 Ph3 topline expected 1Q2026.

sources.name: Johnson & Johnson
sources.title: Icotrokinra Phase 3 ICONIC results (LEAD/ADVANCE/TOTAL)
sources.note: IGA 0/1 ~65–74% at wk24; durable to wk52; scalp/genital efficacy; head-to-head vs ustekinumab initiated.

sources.name: Managed Healthcare Executive
sources.title: Stelara biosimilars launch (2025)
sources.note: US loss of exclusivity 2025; multiple biosimilars (Wezlana, Selarsdi, Pyzchiva, Yesintek, Steqeyma).

sources.name: Bristol Myers Squibb
sources.title: US FDA Approves Sotyktu (deucravacitinib) for Active Psoriatic Arthritis
sources.note: Sotyktu PsA approval (2026); label expansion beyond plaque psoriasis.

disclaimers: Informational only. Not medical or investment advice.
Generated from public data and cited web research as of 2026-07-23; verify before relying on any figure.
Efficacy benchmarks are approximate, drawn from separate pivotal trials with differing populations/designs — cross-trial comparisons are indicative, not head-to-head unless stated.
Sales are reported full-year 2024 unless noted; some figures are global and some US-only per the cited source.
